summary.goal: TBI to four-barrel carburetor swap on a stock 350, then a clean first start.

tasks.task: Pulled the engine and set it on the stand
Bagged and labeled the bell housing bolts

session.notes: Long day. Dad ran the hoist while I steadied it. Block looks solid.

tasks.task: Degreased the block and inspected for cracks
Ran a compression check on the old top end

session.notes: No cracks. Numbers were even enough. Worth the rebuild.

tasks.task: Removed the old throttle body setup

session.notes: Kept the old parts in a bin in case I need to go back.

tasks.task: Started the carburetor teardown on the bench
Photographed every linkage before it came apart

session.notes: Laid the small parts out in a muffin tin so nothing wanders off.

tasks.task: Soaked the carb body, cleaned the passages
Sorted primary and secondary jets, kept them paired

session.notes: One speck of grit ruins a jet, so I worked slow and clean.

tasks.task: Set the new float to the kit gauge spec
Backed the idle mixture screws out about two and a half turns to start

session.notes: Need the bolt pattern confirmed with Jake before the gasket set order.

nextUp.item: Intake gasket set once the bolt pattern is confirmed
Low pressure fuel pump and a carb friendly line
Plugs and wires while the top end is open
Starter motor on the October garage day

openQuestions.question: Will the stock alternator keep up once it runs again?
Reuse the old fuel line or replace the whole run?
